U.S. Limits Intelligence Sharing with South Korea

The United States has restricted satellite intelligence sharing with South Korea after remarks by Seoul’s minister about a suspected North Korean nuclear site.

The development has triggered political backlash in South Korea and raised concerns about trust within the U.S.–South Korea alliance.

South Korean minister Chung Dong-young referenced a uranium enrichment site in Kusong. Washington viewed the remarks as unauthorised disclosure of sensitive intelligence. 

Following this some satellite data sharing has been limited since early April. However, officials say overall military coordination remains unaffected.

Amid chaos and devastation, the Indian Army emerged as the first responder, with troops from the Gorkha Rifles and Dogra Regiment launching an immediate and coordinated rescue operation.

RT by @OsintTV: PAKISTAN ARMY LOOKING FOR WHEELED/TRACKED GUN SYSTEM 

1.  Pakistan Army is looking for induction of SH-16 Gun SYSTEM in its artillery.  

2. Noteworthy is that Pakistan Army is already holding few regiments of Chinese SH-15 Mounted Gun System. During #OpBunyanUmMarsoos Pakistan employed its newly inducted SH-15 MGS. But the Gun sys has not delivered optimum results at maximum range due to heavy dispersion.  But this Gun sys was still better than indigenous FATAH GMLRS since the Fatahs were nearly ineffective however one odd shell of SH-15 landed near intended target. 

3. Based on the lessons learnt,  Pakistan Artillery is now eyeing SH-16 in wheeled & tracked version.  

4. The wheeled version will be employed in area of 10, 30 & 4 Corps while in 31 & 5 Corps the tracked version of SH-16 is likely to be inducted. SH-16 is considered more accurate with Laser Ignition Sys. 

5. A total of approximately one dozen regiments of Pakistan Artillery are supposed to be equipped with SH-15/16. Few regiments (numbers deliberately not exposed) have been inducted with SH-15. Balance regiments are likely to be introduced with SH-16.

RT by @NetramDefence: In Manipur’s difficult terrain, reliable communication is more than a convenience, it is a necessity for daily life. The launch of the Community Radio Station ‘Radio Highway’ (Pallel ki Vaani) by the Assam Rifles at Pallel is a practical step toward strengthening last-mile connectivity.

 Established under Operation Sadbhavana, the station focuses on community empowerment by providing a platform for local voices and sharing vital information on government welfare schemes and weather updates for farming communities.

Significantly, during periods of internet instability or shutdowns, this radio station will serve as a critical, resilient medium for authentic and verified information, ensuring the community remains informed when digital channels are unavailable. It is an effort to bridge the communication gap and support inclusive regional development by the Assam Rifles.
